content.full_text:
RAVE MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
May 12,
1995
Kathleen N. Weinheimer,
Esq.
City Attorney
City of Pismo Beach
1020 Calle Malaga
Santa Barbara, California
93109
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
G-95-155
Dear Ms.
Weinheimer:
You are requesting advice concerning the conflict—of—
interest laws under the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
Your request seeks connuent regarding the disqualification
obligations of two councilmembers for the City of Pismo Beach with
respect to a decision than has already taken place.
We cannot
provide you advice concerning past conduct, unless the assistance
or advice sought is related to possible amendment of previous
reports filed by the person requesting the advice.
(Regulation
18329(c)(4)(A).)
However,
we have enclosed general information
concerning the indirect financial effects of governmental
decisions on real property.
Please note, that this information is
not intended as reaching a conclusion regarding any past decisions
of the City of Pismo Beach.
If you have any questions, please call me at
(916)
322-5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
tZ
1cIc
By:
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division
Enclosures
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
Commission regulations
appear at
2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000
- 18954.
Any references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 3 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: The FPPC cannot provide advice concerning past conduct, unless the assistance sought is related to possible amendment of previous reports filed by the person requesting the advice. General information concerning indirect financial effects of governmental decisions on real property has been enclosed, but this is not intended as reaching a conclusion regarding any past decisions of the City of Pismo Beach.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether two City of Pismo Beach councilmembers had disqualification obligations under the Political Reform Act regarding a decision that had already been made.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to advise on the past disqualification decision, as it generally does not provide counsel on prior conduct unless related to amendment of previously filed reports. General informational materials were provided instead.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The request sought advice on past conduct which the FPPC declined per Regulation 18329(c)(4)(A). Synthetic question and conclusion derived from body text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether two City of Pismo Beach councilmembers had disqualification obligations under the Political Reform Act regarding a decision that had already been made.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide advice concerning past conduct, unless the assistance sought is related to possible amendment of previous reports filed by the person requesting the advice. General information concerning indirect financial effects of governmental decisions on real property has been enclosed, but this is not intended as reaching a conclusion regarding any past decisions of the City of Pismo Beach.
embedding.first_500_words: RAVE MEHTA CHAIRMAN FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION May 12, 1995 Kathleen N. Weinheimer, Esq. City Attorney City of Pismo Beach 1020 Calle Malaga Santa Barbara, California 93109 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. G-95-155 Dear Ms. Weinheimer: You are requesting advice concerning the conflict—of— interest laws under the Political Reform Act (the “Act”). 1 Your request seeks connuent regarding the disqualification obligations of two councilmembers for the City of Pismo Beach with respect to a decision than has already taken place. We cannot provide you advice concerning past conduct, unless the assistance or advice sought is related to possible amendment of previous reports filed by the person requesting the advice. (Regulation 18329(c)(4)(A).) However, we have enclosed general information concerning the indirect financial effects of governmental decisions on real property. Please note, that this information is not intended as reaching a conclusion regarding any past decisions of the City of Pismo Beach. If you have any questions, please call me at (916) 322-5901. Sincerely, Steven G. Churchwell General Counsel tZ 1cIc By: Luisa Menchaca Counsel, Legal Division Enclosures 1 Government Code Sections 81000—91015. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000 - 18954. Any references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 3 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request from the City of Pismo Beach City Attorney for advice on conflict-of-interest disqualification obligations for two councilmembers regarding a decision already made, citing its policy against advising on past conduct.
